name: rebalance-os
description: Your workday OS — surfaces what matters, flags project imbalances, and answers questions about your own work using your Obsidian vault, GitHub activity, and Google Calendar. All local, all on your data.
long_description: rebalance OS ingests your Obsidian vault, GitHub activity, and Google Calendar into a local SQLite database, then lets you ask natural language questions about your own work. It flags over-investment in low-priority projects, surfaces neglected high-priority work, and assembles context for meetings — all running locally on Apple Silicon via MLX.

vault_path.title: Obsidian Vault Path
vault_path.description: The folder where your Obsidian vault lives (contains your .md files)

database_path.title: Database Path
database_path.description: Where to store the rebalance SQLite database

github_token.title: GitHub Personal Access Token
github_token.description: A GitHub PAT with repo:read scope. Create one at github.com/settings/tokens

tools.name: ask
tools.description: Ask any question about your work — combines vault notes, GitHub activity, calendar, and project registry

tools.name: query_notes
tools.description: Semantic search over your Obsidian vault notes

tools.name: github_balance
tools.description: GitHub activity per project with over/under-investment flags

tools.name: query_github_context
tools.description: Semantic search over the local GitHub issue, PR, comment, and commit corpus

tools.name: github_release_readiness
tools.description: Explicit release-readiness inference from the local GitHub corpus

tools.name: github_close_candidates
tools.description: Find open issues that likely map to merged PRs and may be ready to close

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List your tracked projects with priority tiers

tools.name: onboarding_status
tools.description: Check which setup steps are complete

tools.name: setup_github_token
tools.description: Validate and store a GitHub PAT

tools.name: run_preflight
tools.description: Discover project candidates from vault and GitHub

tools.name: confirm_projects
tools.description: Write confirmed projects to the registry

tools.name: create_calendar_event
tools.description: Create a Google Calendar event using the local OAuth token
